title: The One With Rachel's Going Away Party

lines.speaker: Produced by
lines.text: Robert Carlock & Wendy Knoller
Final check by Kim
Joey's place. Rachel and Joey are talking

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, all right, all right, let's play one more time, ok? And remember, if I win you do not move to Paris.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok! Can't believe I'm risking this again, but you're on! All right Joe, you remember the rules! Heads I win, tails you lose.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Just flip!
Rachel (she flips the coin): Ha, tails!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Damnit!
Chandler and Monica enter the room

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So we thought we'd throw you little going away party around seven.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, that sounds good!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, Rach, you're leaving tomorrow, shouldn't you be packing?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's all done!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, yeah, right! And after I took a shower this morning I just threw my towel on the floor! Oh God, it hurts to even joke about it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know... Honey, seriously, I did it all. The luggage that I'm taking is in the bedroom, this is Emma's Paris stuff, these are the boxes that I'm having shipped, and that's the sandwich that I made for the plane...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok, so you've done some good work! (pause) What about your carry-ons?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, well. Everything that I need (she takes her bag) is in here and my travel documents are on the counter organized in the order that I will be needing them.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God! I have nothing left to teach you! (they hug)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Where's your passport?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It should be right next to my plane ticket.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, it's not.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? Maybe I put it in here (she opens her bag). Oh, oh, it's not in there! Oh, no! I must have packed it in one of these boxes!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Here, let me help you. (they both start opening boxes)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Shoot. Oh, I can't believe I did this!
Chandler (to Joey): At what point did it stop being funny that I took her passport?
OPENING SEQUENCE
Monica's apartment. Monica and Erica are talking about the baby, and Monica is rubbing Erica's tummy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, wow, can you believe you're like three weeks away?

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You don't mind me touching your belly, do you?

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: No, I don't mind you touching my belly, but right now your hand is kind of blocking the part where the baby is gonna come out.
She takes her hand off Erica. Ross enters the room

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey Erica, welcome back to town! (pause) Wow, look how big you've gotten.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: That's because I'm pregnant!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Right, no, I understand.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Oh, ok. I'm just always afraid that people think I'm just fat with big breasts.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, no, I knew (he stares at her breasts).
Monica (to Ross): Okay, well, stop staring at them.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: She brought them up! (pause) I didn't realize you were coming back so soon!

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Hey, well, in a couple of weeks I won't be able to travel.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, and I wanted her to get to know the doctors and get settled into the hotel.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hotel? Why isn't she staying with you guys?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Because we're moving in a couple of days and it just didn't make sense.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Plus hotels are fun! My room has this little fridge full of free snacks!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Erica, those things aren't free. In fact they have one of the highest mark-ups of any consumer product...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross! She's giving us her baby. She can eat you if she wants.
Erica (standing up): I'll be right back.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh man, I can't believe you guys are leaving this place.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, I know. I know. Hey, you know, you can take it if you want! The lease is still in Nana's name.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, no. This will always be your place. It would be too sad. Plus, how much a month does it cost to feed Joey?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, it takes two incomes.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, is Chandler here? We talked about catching a movie.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, no. He doesn't have time for that. But if you want, you can go help him and Joey pack up the guest room.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Mhm, (he balance things) packing - sexy cheerleader comedy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Mhm, helpful brother - creepy loner at teen movie.
Ross takes the tape roll she's handing him and walks to the guest room while mocking Monica's voice
Guest room. Joey has his head wrapped in bubbled wrap and Chandler is punching him. Ross enters the room.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What are you guys doing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Try it, I can't feel a thing! (Ross starts punching him too)
Monica (enters the room): Are, are you kidding? This is packing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We're taking a break!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: From?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Jumping on the bed?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, Rachel's party is in a couple of hours and there's a lot to do. Now, Ross, you got Geller blood, you're in charge of these yahoos!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You got it! (Monica leaves, Ross closes the door). All right, she's right, we gotta get serious. (He grabs a bag of styrofoam peanuts) Let's put styrofoam peanuts down his pants and kick him!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, no, no, guys. She's right. We should get to work. I'll take stuff out of the closet, Joey you pack 'em and Ross you re-pack whatever Joey packs.
Joey takes the bubble wrap off his head

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You guys hear a ringing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (holding a pair of furry handcuffs) What the hell is this?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! Handcuffs! And fur line, nice! I didn't know you guys had it in ya!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Chandler, you don't have a sister so you can't understand how much this bums me out.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I didn't know Monica had these!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Mhm, maybe she used them with another boyfriend. Maybe Richard!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why would she use them with Richard and not me? I can be kinky! I once did a naked dance for her... with scarves!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Bumming hard, guys, bumming hard.
Monica's apartment. Phoebe and Ross enter the room.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey! Where's Mike?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, he has a gig. I kinda like being married to a rock star, you know. My husband has a gig.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah? Yeah, where is it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, he's playing organ for a children's roller-skating party.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rock on!
Phoebe (watching the food on the table): Wow, this is quite a spread! (pause) What is all this stuff?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, I thought this would be a great opportunity to use up all the food that I don't want to move to the new house with me! So, enjoy: smoke oyster casserole with a breakfast cereal crust, kidney beans in their own juices, and for dessert, a questionable orange.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering the room) Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, where's Rach?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, she's putting Emma down, she'll be over in a second.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Great.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Now it just hit her that she's leaving and she's kind of emotional so no one say anything to set her off, ok?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah.
Rachel enters the room

lines.speaker: Everybody
lines.text: Hey Rach, hey you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Here she is!
Rachel (crying): Hi you guys!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What did I just say?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, no, no. It's ok. I'm gonna be fine.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come here, I'll make you a drink.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, man! I can't believe she's actually leaving. How am I gonna say goodbye to Rachel?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I know, she's been such a big part of my life. And it feels like when Melrose Place got cancelled. (Ross and Joey looks puzzled) I mean... oh, forget it. I miss Melrose Place!
Joey (to Ross): You know, I had a chance to stop her too!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Who loses fifty seven coin tosses in a row? Head she wins, tails I lose. (he stops and starts realizing something) Wait a minute...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, Joe?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I forgot to pick up my dry cleaning!
Phoebe (to Rachel): You doing ok?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I've been better.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-hmm.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You guys are gonna come and visit me, right?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes! You know, in six months the Statute of Limitations runs out and I can travel internationally again!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm gonna miss you so much.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You know what? Uhm, I have some goodbye stuff that I wanted to say to each of you and I was gonna save it until the end of the night, but come here (they go into the guest room).

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, what do you think they're doing in there?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Huh, if I had to guess I'd say Rachel is putting on the bubble wrap and Phoebe is doing the punching.
Guest room. Rachel and Phoebe are sitting on the bed.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, Pheebs, I don't even know where to start.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok well, before you do, I know we weren't supposed to get you going away presents, (she takes something out from her pocket) but I do have something for you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, oh. (she's holding the present, a transparent bag with a white stick in it). What is this?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's a cotton swab with a bit of my saliva on it, so that if they perfect the cloning process while you are over there, you can use the DNA to create your own Pheebs!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm gonna throw this away, but thank you so much for the gesture!
Monica's apartment. The living room.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler? I was just in our bedroom and I found these (she holds the furry handcuffs) on my pillow.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, yes. I decided to leave these out for you in case Richard stops by and you wanna engage on a little light bondage and moustache play!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you talking about? These aren't mine.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah, right! Good luck getting another scarf dance from me!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That would be a terrible punishment. But, I'm serious, I've never seen these before.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really? Then what are they doing in our guestroom?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rachel used to live in that room.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Rachel... with handcuffs! Interesting! (he looks excited)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey's bare ass!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (he doesn't look excited anymore). Well played.
Phoebe and Rachel enter the living room.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I love you Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I love you too. (they hug) Please don't... Don't turn into... you know... French bitch! (they hug again)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right. Well, if I gonna do this, I'd better keep going.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok. Monica?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Can you come here with me for a minute?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Monica) Are you wearing waterproof mascara?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, you're so screwed. (Monica goes into the guest room)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Phoebe) Hey. What was that all about?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: She's gonna say goodbye to each of us individually.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Are you kidding? Oh my God...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I know, it's gonna be even worse for you... God... Ross, get ready to do some serious crying.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, man, I'm not going to be able to handle this. (pause) Now I know how my students feel at the end of each year. And why they act out by giving me such bad evaluations.
The guest bedroom. Rachel and Monica are talking to each other.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Mon... Okay... I've gotta... just say what it is I'm gonna say... None of the amazing things that have happened to me in the last ten years, would have happened if it wasn't for you. No-one has been more like a sister to me...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know what you mean. You're like a sister to me too.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (starts crying and speaking at the same time, making it almost impossible to understand what she's saying) I wouldn't know what I'm gonna do without you...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (having the same problem) You're the best friend I ever had.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (says something that cannot be understood)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I... I... I... (again saying something that cannot be understood)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That is so sweet. (they hug)
Back to the living room. Monica and Rachel enter and hug each other. The guys see this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Joey) Oh no, she took down Monica... And I'm the crier in the family. Oh God! I could be next. Maybe she won't talk with me if it looks like we're deep in converstation. Oh, so that thing you said about the thing. It really made me think about that other thing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh, Rach?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well it's okay. Chandler is talking to her.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I really made you think about that thing uh?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Rachel) Uh, Rach... I think I have something that belongs to you. (shows her the cuffs)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (laughs) Oh, I'm sure gonna miss pretending to laugh at your weird jokes that I don't get.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, no, no... They're really yours. We... found them in your old room.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, these aren't mine. Maybe Monica used to use them with...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Don't say Richard! Well, if they're not Monica's and they're not yours, then whose are they?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I think you're forgetting the kinkiest former resident of that room.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Pheebs!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (from the other side of the room) Yeah!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I think these are yours.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: These are not mine... Look how flimsy they are, come on! Good God! You try to hang a guy from a waterpipe with these, they'll snap like a piece of licorice.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Chandler) Can I talk to you alone for a minute?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sure. (to Phoebe) You don't really handcuff guys to waterpipes do you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Where do you think Mike really is? (she giggles, Chandler looks aghast)
The guest bedroom. Chandler and Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh honey...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Let me just say something... Because once we get into this, I'm gonna get all uncomfortable and probably make some stupid joke... I just want to say that I... I love you... And, I'm gonna miss you. And I'm so sad that you're leaving.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (all mushy) Oh, you know what? Let's not say anything else. I love you. (they hug)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ooh, not so tight... (blows raspberry, and the hug ends) I'm sorry, just give me one more chance.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Oh...
Chandler blows raspberry again

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm sor... Just go. Just go. I can't, I can't.
Monica's living room. We look outside to the balcony where Rachel is saying goodbye to Joey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Ross) So, I guess you're next. You're ready?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, you're definately not. I haven't cried like that in years.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You cried yesterday at the six o'clock news.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That old woman was being scammed by her mechanic.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: God! I can't believe she saved me for last. (looking out to the balcony) Why are they taking so long?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don't know. But, God, Joey seems to be handling it suprisingly well.
Rachel and Joey hug, and Rachel turns to enter the apartment through the side window again. At that moment Joey tries to climb over the edge of the balcony.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: No, no, no, no!
Rachel rushes back and pulls Joey back onto the balcony, and takes him inside.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, here we go...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh... (holding Ross's shoulder) Well...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I think I'm gonna take off. (pats Ross on his back, but he looks very surprised)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, you guys. This was an amazing night. Thank you so much. I love you. Good night.
She leaves the apartment and they all stare at Ross

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? I don't get a goodbye?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (still very emotional) Lucky bastard!
Monica's apartment continued... Phoebe, Chandler, Monica and Joey are sitting down and Ross is pacing up and down.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Unbelievable. She says goodbye to everyone but me.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, maybe she thought that with all of your history it could be, you know, implicit.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, it needs to be plicit.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, let's think about this. I mean, there's got to be an explanation. Uh... did you do anything to make her mad?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You know, maybe she was just really spent from our talk. It was pretty intense.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah. Mine too.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Mine was a humdinger

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (annoyed) O-kay... I mean, don't I deserve anything? I mean, a few tears, a cursory hug? (Joey gives Ross a hug) NOT FROM YOU! (Joey lets go)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ross, if you're this upset, you should go and talk to her.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And say what? "You owe me a goodbye", I mean, he's got more pride than that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: THE HELL I DO!
Ross takes big steps leaving for Joey and Rachel's apartment, where Rachel is going through her papers.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't get a goodbye?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (talking agitated and angry) Everyone gets a goodbye but me? What have I got to do to get a goodbye, huh? Be best friends with you? Uh, go out with you? Have a baby with you? Oh wait a minute, wait a minute, I did all those things.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh no, maybe it's me, I'm just not giving you enough credit. Uh, I mean it is difficult to say goodbye to five people. Uh, goodbye, goodbye, goodbye, goodbye, good... (makes choking noises) IT'S PHYSICALLY IMPOSSIBLE. You know what? After all we've been through, I can't believe this is how you want to leave things between us. Have a, have a good time in Paris. (He leaves the apartment. Rachel looks kind of desperate.)
Monica and Chandler's apartment. The others are still there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I hope Ross isn't too upset.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm sure he's not more bummed out than I am.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Tell me about it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, you can't say we don't know how to throw a party.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, I think I'm gonna head out.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh, where do you think you're going?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I thought I was going home to go to bed, but I'm sensing there's something less fun for me to do here.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We're moving in a couple of days and we've got a lot of packing to do. It would be great if you guys could pitch in.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joey and I can finish up in the guest room.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, yeah, yeah, good idea. (he mimes hitting his head with his fist)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh no! You and Phoebe are gonna help me in here.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Joey) You couldn't be cool. (he goes to the guest bedroom)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, we're gonna start in the kitchen. Plates get put into plate protectors and stacked ten to a box. The silverware gets bundled in rubber bands and then bubble wrapped. Got it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe and Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Good! Now I need you to be careful and efficient. And remember, if I am harsh with you, it is only because you are doing it wrong.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey Mon, I think I figured out whose handcuffs they are.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You did? How?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I was cleaning out the closet and I found some pictures of them... being used.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God! Let me see. (they all look at the pictures)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Who's that dirty old lady?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Monica's grandmother.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nana liked it rough!
Erica, the pregnant girl, enters

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on here, have a seat.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: How was your night?

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Oh, it was okay. I went to a movie with my cousin and then out for dinner. We went to this place that had... Ooh... (she looks likes she is in pain, holding her belly) Hoo... ooh... Anyway, they had these really amazing cheeseburgers.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Erica, are you okay?

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: Yeah, you know, maybe I ate too much. I keep getting these stomach aches. They come and go like every few minutes.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Relax! We just get her some antacids.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: She doesn't have a stomach ache, she's in labor.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, you got to get to the hospital.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, Chandler get the coats. Erica let's go. Phoebe and Joey, keep packing! Oh my God we're gonna have a baby. All right. We're gonna have a baby! OH MY GOD, WE'RE GONNA HAVE A BABY! Oh God, oh God, I got to sit down, I got to sit down. Ooh! (she's hyperventilating)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Honey, it's gonna be okay.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: You can do this. Just breathe.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, okay... Okay, I feel a little better.

lines.speaker: Erica
lines.text: (in pain) Ooh! Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh... Yes, I'm sure. Oh honey, let's go. Okay bye everyone.
there's a lot of supportive cheers from all. Erica, Monica and Chandler leave.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Chandler, wait, wait, wait...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: If you get a second, find out where she got that cheeseburger.
Ross's apartment. Rachel bursts in.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You really think I didn't say goodbye to you because I don't care?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's what it seemed like.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I cannot believe that after ten years, you do not know ONE thing about me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Fine, then why didn't you say something?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Because it is too damn hard Ross. I can't even begin to explain to you how much I'm gonna miss you. When I think about not seeing you every day, it makes me not want to go... Okay, so if you think that I didn't say goodbye to you because you don't mean as much to me as everybody else, you're wrong. It's because you mean more to me. So there, all right, there's your goodbye... Oh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rach!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You keep, you keep... You can't...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: WHAT?
Ross walks over to her and starts to kiss her passionately. After a while Rachel backs out. She thinks a while and starts kissing him back.
COMMERCIAL BREAK
Chandler and Monica's apartment.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: A little more.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. (she starts to stuff styrofoam peanuts down the front of Joey's pants.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: A little more, a little more. (she stuffs some more down his pants and Joey's assesses the how many there already are in there.) A little more... All right. Okay, all right, let's do this.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, ready?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. All right, now give me your best shot.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, here it goes...
she knees him in the crotch. He jumps up a bit

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (in pain) Doesn't work... (he falls down on on his knees with his head on the floor)
THE END
